currentTarget.statement: Candidate formalization target: Prove or package the helper stack cleanly and checkpoint the route if the leaf closes.
currentTarget.whyThisExists: This packet exists to turn a claim-pass recommendation into a concrete theorem-facing proof obligation with explicit falsifiers and promotion criteria.
currentTarget.whyNow: Prove or package the helper stack cleanly and checkpoint the route if the leaf closes.
evidenceBasis: No canonical verified interval is currently frozen.

proofObligations.task: State the candidate theorem-facing claim in pack language without overstating current support.
proofObligations.why: This keeps the formalization target narrower than the current evidence boundary.
proofObligations.dischargeWith: Write a claim statement that is no stronger than the active claim-pass support surface.

proofObligations.task: List the exact canonical artifacts that support the claim and the artifacts that could still falsify it.
proofObligations.why: The theorem lane should point at stable evidence, not drifting interpretation.
proofObligations.dischargeWith: Attach theorem-loop, claim-pass, and any bridge-backed packet paths directly to the packet.

proofObligations.task: Promote the target only when its support survives the current claim-pass falsifiers.
proofObligations.why: This prevents formalization packets from silently outrunning the rest of the loop.
proofObligations.dischargeWith: Record explicit promotion criteria tied to current falsifiers.

falsifierChecks.task: A refreshed claim pass drops the target recommendation or marks the underlying claim unsupported.
falsifierChecks.why: If the recommendation itself no longer survives refresh, the formalization target is stale.

falsifierChecks.task: A canonical artifact boundary check shows the statement is stronger than the current pack evidence.
falsifierChecks.why: The packet should fail early if it is stronger than what the pack actually certifies.

promotionCriteria.task: The current target remains the top theorem-formalization recommendation after refresh.
The candidate statement is narrower than or equal to the current claim-pass support surface.
The remaining proof obligations are explicit enough to guide search or exact follow-up.

The pack already carries a frontier note that can anchor theorem-facing updates.
